description: 'thunaro' guides someone through structured practice towards a skill or stated criterion. Training may include demonstration and repeated attempts with feedback, but it does not promise that learning occurred. 'thumela' (teach) is broader, while 'thole' (practice) names the learner's repeated work.
articulatory_notes: Start with unvoiced dental 'th', passing breath through the narrow opening at the upper teeth into high-back rounded 'u'. Return the tongue tip for voiced dental 'n' and carry nasal airflow into stressed central 'a'. For final 'ro', strike the alveolar ridge once or trill there briefly before true-mid back rounded 'o'.
sound_symbolism: Dental breath gives way to the steadier nasal centre of stressed 'na', then final 'ro' turns at the tongue. The sequence has the measured change of one attempt followed by another kind of guidance.
usage_notes: The learner is the direct object and precedes 'thunaro'; the event-noun rule supplies training. The skill, method, conditions, and criterion can appear nearby. Agreement to one session or method does not settle later participation, so questions and refusal remain ordinary parts of the relation.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Training keeps the learner's questions and refusal available instead of turning guidance into discipline or certification.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Structured practice lets an experienced hand demonstrate pressure, timing, and material response that written instruction may not carry.

semantic_domains.activity: guiding repeated practice towards a practical capacity
semantic_domains.cognition: learning organised through demonstration and feedback
semantic_domains.community: a bounded relation between trainer and learner
